response.step_by_step_log: 1. Identified song title 'ray' from on-screen text. 2. Identified performer as Kazama Iroha from concert tags. 3. Confirmed 'ray' is by BUMP OF CHICKEN. 4. Searched for lyrics for 'ray' by BUMP OF CHICKEN. 5. Mapped video timeline to song performance (until 03:04.000) and talking/announcement (03:04.000-end). 6. Compiled lyrics into required format.
response.global_summary: A video featuring a live performance of the song 'ray' by Kazama Iroha, followed by talking and audience interaction.

scenes.description: Kazama Iroha performs the song 'ray' on a stage with a virtual concert background.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
It seems like it was a long time ago that we said goodbye
I’ve been worn out on my heels, keeping the sad light inside
When I was with you I could see it, now I can’t
I’m looking for a comet, a transparent comet, but that’s all I’m looking for
I often sang a song, a melody just for that moment
I wasn’t lonely, I could be lonely
I don’t even have time to think about how far I’ve gone, about what’s normal or abnormal
It’s so hard to walk that I don’t even have time to think about it
It’s much better to have fun, I’ll just fake it and smile
Don’t worry, that pain won’t go away even if you forget
The reality will rewrite the road we made with our ideals
Memories will shine brightly on the path
I wonder why and for what I said goodbye
The sad light stretches my shadow long before me
Sometimes I get a fever, when I have time, I sleep
In the midst of what I know to be a dream, I’ll see you and then go again
The sky is far from clear, even in the never-ending darkness
If I think of stars, I’ll soon be in the galaxy
I’ll cry less, I’ll get new shoes
Don’t worry, that pain won’t go away even if you forget
I’m sure there’s something you wanted to tell me
I know it’s probably a cliché, but I’ve never had to say goodbye like this
What we said goodbye to is connected to what we met
That transparent comet will never disappear because it’s transparent
Circle, x, triangle, whichever, you can compare yourself to others
I’m so happy to be alive that I don’t even have time to check if I’m fat, x, or y
Even if I don’t cry much anymore, I’ll just fake it and smile
Don’t worry, that pain won’t go away even if you forget
Don’t worry, you’re here at the beginning of this light

scenes.description: Kazama Iroha speaks to the audience and talks about the concert.
